Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are going to construct a Petri net for the described insurance claim process. Steps: 1. Define the Petri net and its name. 2. Create places for each state between activities and the start and end. Then the steps are sequential: CR DV FC CA AP P N CL But note: there are loops for RDS and II.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: start representing the beginning of the process new claim arrives. Option 1: start CR place1 CR. We can model the claim filing as a transition? Or model the claim filing as the initial state a token in a place representing the claim filed and then the first transition is Claim Registration CR.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The initial marking should place a token in a start place representing a new claim arriving. A customer files an Insurance Claim C after which the following steps take place starting with Claim Registration CR. So start new claim arriving after being filed then the first activity is Claim Registration CR.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: After CR we go to DV. Then after DV if documents are incomplete we go to RDS Re-Document Submission and then back to DV. Similarly for Fraud Check FC if doubts II In-depth Investigation and then back to FC. This can happen multiple times before proceeding to Claim Assessment CA.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Approval AP The claim must be approved by a senior manager if the amount surpasses a certain threshold. We might have to model two possibilities but the problem does not specify the condition so we assume that the AP step is always executed. Model AP as a transition that is always taken.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The main sequence is CR DV loop RDS FC loop II CA AP P N CL. Define the places: start after_CR after_DV after_FC after_CA after_AP after_P after_N after_CL. Transitions: CR DV RDS FC II CA AP P N CL.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Model the loop by after_DV RDS after_DV loop. after_DV FC after_FC. Similarly after_FC II after_FC loop. after_FC CA after_CA. In Petri nets a place with two outgoing transitions RDS and FC is a choice determined by condition.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Arcs: start CR after_CR after_CR DV after_DV after_DV RDS after_DV after_DV FC after_FC after_FC II after_FC after_FC CA after_CA after_CA AP after_AP after_AP P after_P after_P N after_N after_N CL after_CL. Initial marking token in start. Final marking token in after_CL.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Steps in code: 1. Import necessary modules. 2. Create a PetriNet object. 3. Create the places and add them to the net. 4. Create the transitions and add them to the net. 5. Add arcs from places to transitions and from transitions to places. 6. Define the initial and final markings.
